name: Murdock, Seth
clubs: Y. M. C. A. entire time in school
description: "Murdy" is one of our social leaders and also quite an athlete. He is one of the best students in school and is very popular at the Girl's "Dom." This flaxen-haired youth is evidently planning a very bright future for himself for Gilliam says that he is awakened frequently by Seth's raving in his sleep about the beautiful country home and the rosy-cheeked, blue-eyed maiden who, we all know, he some day expects to call his own. Intellect, talent and genius, Like murder, will out, With the ambition of Venus He lingers about.

name: Souter, Fairy W.
description: "Willie" is a native of Magnolia. He heard of the wonderful advantages and opportunities offered by the S. D. A., so lost no time in coming to us. That he is a born leader is shown by the fact that he was elected captain of "Scientific Tommie's" baseball team in the spring of 1916, and as captain of the football team of 1916 he successfully led the men to victory. We are not sure just what he intends to do when he gets through school, but we believe that he will go home and raise Johnson grass or some other crop which requires very little labor. There was only one window in the Ark, But it opened in the right direction; So of all who came out thereof, We have this great selection.

name: Duff, Coventon
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A. 1915
description: Duff is in the German class and seems to be very fond of "German." She has learned to say, "Ich Liebe Dich" already. That Duff has a mind of her own we all agree; and to try to make her do a thing which she thinks is not the right thing to do would be as useless as for a man to try to make the earth revolve from east to west. If anyone wonders why the boys are afraid of her, just look at her jet black eyes and the question is answered. A miniature of sweetness, A genius of neatness, Always so prim With knowledge to the brim.

name: Mayme, Evans
clubs: Member Y. W. C. A. entire time in school
description: "Shrimp" is a beautiful blonde with a most amiable disposition. Her friends may be counted by the score. She is always bright and happy and has cheered us through the many storms which have come to our Senior year. Mayme is especially fond of history—especially "Shinn's" History. There is one thing, however, which is not clear to us—why does she say "Dixon Mason" instead of Mason-Dixon? We are sure that all the histories that we have ever read call it Mason-Dixon. There is ever a soft and pensive grace, A cast of thought on her sweet face. What, if in pleasure she did roam? On April the first, we found her at home.

name: Wilson, Guy
motto: "His thoughts are his own companions. He is a genius in every-day life. None but himself can be his parallel, That is no mystery if strife."
description: "Pete" is a very quiet, studious, good natured boy who never says anything at class meeting, except when he wishes to disagree. That he is not afraid of hard studies is shown by the fact that he has taken Entomology, Plant Diseases, Geometry, and Chemistry without a quiver and has passed them all with a good grade. He is not especially fond of feminine society but does not seem to be afraid of girls (at a distance.) Hendrix or the U. of A. will probably be fortunate enough to have him next year.

name: Reed, J. S.
motto: "One of the best uses of originality is to say Common things in an uncommon way. Things that are common are often said, But none can say them as can this lad."
clubs: Davis Debating Club entire time in school
description: From the first Joe has been an excellent student, but believes that "all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy." He enjoys "stacking" rooms, or going to some other boy's room and "raising a rough house." He is from that famous fruit growing section in northwest Arkansas, but seems to be more interested in Animal Husbandry than in Horticulture. We believe that he will some day be one of Arkansas' most prominent breeders.

name: John, Page
clubs: Vice President 1915, Secretary 1916, and President second term 1917 of Periclean Literary Society
Vice President, 1915, Secretary, 1917 of Davis Debating Club
Sugar Stealer of Hot Chocolate Club 1916-17
description: From the first John's career has been a brilliant one. As you can see from his record above, he has taken an active part in societies and clubs. He is a good student and a loyal supporter of our school. His favorite pastime is making chocolate. His next few years will probably be spent in one of our best Agricultural Colleges, then he will go home, find a "jewel," and win the reputation of being one of Arkansas' best farmers. Here we see a cavalier Of dashing grace and countenance gay, A well tried hero without a fear, The girls all love him so they say.

name: Loyd, Gilliam
clubs: Vice President of Davis Debating Club, 1914-15
President of Davis Debating Club, fall term, 1916
Vice President of Periclean Literary Society, fall term, 1916
President of Y. M. C. A., 1916-17
description: Without a doubt "Two and Two" will prepare for the bar. The oratory displayed by him in the Literary Society, Debating Club, etc., and his never ceasing arguments in class and in the dormitory, have fully proven to us that nature intended that he should be a lawyer. After graduating from S. D. A., he will go to Washington and Lee University, and take the law course offered there. We believe that he will not be satisfied with that but will go higher before settling down to his chosen occupation of practicing law. A thrifty man suspects himself a fool; At twenty, knows it and reforms. Good, better, best, never lets it rest, Until the good is better, and the better is best.

name: Lippeatt, Esther Galloway
description: Esther knew that she was to be the mistress of a home in the near future, and as she wished to adorn her home with those things found in the home of only expert seamstresses, and to furnish her husband with a properly cooked and balanced ration, she came to S. D. A. to take a course in Domestic Art and Domestic Science. It is rumored that she is already married, and if this be true, we extend to her our heartfelt sympathies and wish her that which is always wished, but which never comes true, to those whose heart Cupid's dart hath pierced—Happiness. This young lady is very talented, She has all kinds of little nooks; If you ever want to know anything, Ask her, she's a walking book.

name: Brown, Cecile
motto: "It is better to be than to appear to be."
clubs: Vice President of Y. W. C. A., 1915
description: Cecile is a small, precise young lady who does her own thinking and who always does what she thinks is right, regardless of consequences. She does not say much, but when she does say something we always know it is something worth hearing. She wears a never-fading smile, is always bright and cheerful, and ready to lend her assistance to those who need help. What though she may be filled with sorrow She never saves her smile for tomorrow.

name: Bullock, Mary E.
motto: "Tis better to speak plainly and have folks understand it, Than to run behind the stump and have them growl about it."
description: Mary says that the most important office she has held since she has been with us, was that of "serving stolen chickens to W. A. Cazort, Jr. and clan at table No. 1, in 1914-'15." She has been here three successive years and has proved to be a good student. She has been faithful to every duty and loyal to S. D. A. and the Class of '17. We regret, for the welfare of our school, that girls like Mary must graduate.

name: Adams, Ell
motto: 'Tis ever the way of the foolish fair; To die for the one who doesn't give a care.
description: One day a long freight stopped at Russellville, and from off the rods of a large box car slid a cinder-covered hobo. He saw a crowd of young men and women going north from the depot, so followed the crowd and came to S. D. A. Instinctively, the kitchen door of the "Beanery" was the first place that he stopped. He liked Mr. Rowden's cooking so well, that he decided to stay a while. It was indeed fortunate for us that the freight stopped at Russellville, for Ell has been one of the best students that the school has ever had. The education he received before he started "ramblin'," together with the knowledge gained in practical life, has enabled him to win his B. S. degree in two years. Ell has seen all of North America, and would have traveled in Europe, if freight trains could have crossed the ocean.

name: Bell, Hazel Jewell
motto: If to her lot some feminine errors fall, Look on her face; you will forget them all. She is all my fancy painted her. She is lovely; she's divine.
description: Here you see a charming maiden, who is a 1916 graduate of Russellville High School. She has accomplished what very few girls have done—completed the Domestic Science and the Domestic Art courses offered at S. D. A. in one year. Although she has had a very heavy schedule, she has found time to join us in our Class picnics and Socials (alias Shindigs.) Memories of her will ever be present when we think of the happy days spent in our Senior year at S. D. A.

name: Prettyman, Keith C.
motto: "Play when you work, and play when you play; That is the way to be happy and gay. Never do today what you can put off until tomorrow."
description: It is rumored that he is editor of the Aggie News, and judging from the wit contained in this twice-a-week record of the proceedings around "Aggieville," we do not believe the rumor to be wholly untrue. "Eggs" is not a regular visitor at the Girl's "Dorm" but he seems to be very popular with the town girls. He has specialized in Horticulture, and next year will go to the already famous northwest Arkansas and put into practice what he has learned while in S. D. A.

name: Patterson, J. Bert
motto: "Good nature is one of the richest gifts of Christianity, Faithfulness is the soul of goodness; Intellect and courage, the gift of humanity, A smile or kind word is cheer for sadness."
description: "Pat" usually has very little to say if girls are present, but when out with the boys he is a jolly good fellow and believes in having all the fun possible. He has the tenacity of a bulldog. Whether in class or on the athletic field he never gives up. If he makes an attempt and fails, he is always ready to try harder the next time. In football a man was never too large to hit, and hit hard. He believes in attending to his own business and letting the other fellow do the same.

name: Williamson, Charles W.
motto: "The noblest lesson taught in life To every great heroic soul, Who seeks to conquer in the strife, Is self control."
description: "Chally" is continually tangled in Cupid's web. It sometimes looks as if he is going to be freed, but as soon as he gets loose from one web, he finds himself more deeply entangled in another. He has been one of the leaders of his class during his entire school career. He always greets you with a smile, and his generous, genial manners have won for him many friends while here in school.

name: Brown, Hardy H.
motto: "We've laughed with glee for three and a half years At this boy, whose smile touches both of his ears."
description: "NAVY" is one of the Russellville boys who intends to be a tiller of the soil, and who realizes the importance of studying scientific agriculture. His wit and friendly manner have won him many friends and made him a favorite of the Senior class. We all believe that "Navy" helps publish the Aggie News. He would probably make a more brilliant success as a comedian than as a farmer.

name: Hill, Agnes B.
clubs: Secretary of Y. W. C. A.
description: She is the youngest member of the Class of '17. We are glad of the honor to have this charming young lady in our class. Without her the year would not have been complete. She is refined and jolly, and her everlasting smile and even temperament have won for her many life-long friends. Those who paint her truest praise her most. For kind hearts are more than coronets, And simple faith than Norman blood.

name: Steuber, Herman George
description: He came to us as a Freshman and has successfully completed the full four-year course. He is not especially fond of Geometry, Chemistry, and Entomology, but for Mechanics and Music we have never found his equal. He knows a gas engine from the batteries to the exhaust. We have never seen a musical instrument that he could not play. In 1915 he made a violin, and within a month was able to play some of the most difficult pieces. He is said to be the best "tooter" of the Trombone between Ft. Smith and Little Rock, and it is doubtful if his equal could be found in Arkansas.

name: Bowden, James L.
motto: Purpose, faith, hope and charity, Truthfulness, honesty, and perseverance Are seven pillars of the character of life. For to live is not merely to exist.
description: Magazine is responsible for the photograph which now confronts you. He came to us when but a loud-mouthed boy wearing knee pants. He did not come back for the 1915-16 term but realizing what a year out of school meant, he entered promptly last fall determining to make this, his last, the best of his three years spent in S. D. A. He is by nature a lover of good livestock and we believe it will be a matter of only a few years before he will own one of the best herds of beef cattle in America, and his name will go down on record as one of America's most famous breeders.

name: Hale, Olsen O.
motto: Men of few words are best they say, For silence is the most perfect herald of joy. But ere the closing of the day In class we heard silence annoyed.
clubs: President, second term, Davis Debating Club, 1916-17
description: Hale is one of the strongest graduates ever winning a B. S. degree from S. D. A. He is a clear thinker and is considered by many to be the best student in school. He has been a zealous worker in clubs and literary societies from the time he first entered school; and his influence will be missed very much next year by those who take part in these organizations. He has majored in Agronomy, and we hope he will be able to supply Uncle Sam with enough "Aggie" Beans next year to feed the army until the war ends.

name: Jackson, Bernice
description: Although this young lady is a resident of Durant, Oklahoma, she is by birth an Arkansas. She spent her childhood days at Magazine, Arkansas, and still considers Arkansas her home. Some day one of our Arkansas farmers will go to Durant and bring back with him her, who belongs to Arkansas. It is well for us that Bernice did not come back for the 1915-16 term, for she has been of invaluable help to us this year.

name: Cazort, Wakefield Anthony
clubs: Periclean Literary Society, entire time in school
Y. M. C. A., entire time in school
Most Royal Master, Royal Sons of O'Syrus, 1916-17
description: He is one of our best all-round men and we all like him. He has been with us ever since the beginning of our class and is a loyal supporter of S. D. A. He is an athlete of no little distinction and since he has been with us has won for himself and for the school many honors. He stands for a square deal and fair play in every game. His favorite pastime is making hot chocolate and "entertaining the Profs." He cares not for the Fair Sex, and doesn't even believe the boys should ever go near the Girl's "Dorm." He regrets to think that the world will go on; After all have declared him dead; That lovers will go on loving, And the same old words be said.
